title: Contribution Margin — Per Unit & Ratio
event: Reyes Co. sells a product for $50 per unit with variable costs of $30 per unit. Compute the contribution margin per unit and the contribution margin ratio.

computationPaths.narration: Contribution margin is what each unit contributes toward covering fixed costs — and after break-even, toward profit.

memorize.body: CM per unit = price − variable cost per unit. CM ratio = CM ÷ price.
Fixed costs don't appear in CM at all — that's the point. CM measures what's LEFT to pay them.

distractors.feedback: That's the variable-cost ratio (30/50). CM is the other side: 20/50 = 40%.
That's CM per UNIT in dollars. The RATIO divides by price: 40%.
